senses.definition: to restore or create a new canal or channel in a body of water
examples: The engineer proposed to recanalise the river to improve navigation.
After the flood, authorities began to recanalise sections of the estuary.

senses.definition: having had a river or waterway restored or newly created through canalisation
examples: The recanalised sections now support better boat traffic.
Engineers reported that the recanalised channels reduced flooding.

senses.definition: third person singular of recanalise; restores or creates a canal or waterway
examples: The project recanalises several minor streams to improve irrigation.
Officials recanalises the old harbor to accommodate larger ships.

senses.definition: present participle of recanalise; in the process of restoring or creating a canal or waterway
examples: The city is recanalising the river to boost tourism.
Work is ongoing in recanalising the marshes into usable channels.

senses.definition: the act or process of creating or restoring a canal or waterway
examples: The recanalization of the river improved irrigation and navigation.
Costs for recanalization are included in the project budget.

senses.definition: plural of recanalization; multiple instances of canal restoration or creation
examples: The report details several recanalizations across the delta.
Funding is allocated to several recanalizations this year.

senses.definition: to reopen or re-create a canal or waterway; to divert or channel flow through a new or restored channel
examples: They plan to recanalize the river to restore navigation.
Experts propose to recanalize parts of the estuary.

senses.definition: having undergone the process of recanalization
examples: The recanalized river base now supports new navigation routes.
After recanalized works, the harbor was opened to small vessels.

senses.definition: third person singular present of recanalize
examples: The project recanalizes several streams to improve drainage.
Officials recanalizes the harbor for larger ships.

senses.definition: present participle of recanalize; in the process of canal restoration or creation
examples: The city is recanalizing parts of the river for leisure boats.
Recanalizing the channel is a key part of the flood-control plan.

senses.definition: to cancel again or to revoke a previously cancelled action or event
examples: They decided to recancel the conference due to weather.
The plan was recancelled after new information emerged.

senses.definition: having had a previous cancellation reversed or reversed cancellation
examples: The trip was recanceled and then rescheduled.
After the update, the recanceled event was reinstated.

senses.definition: present participle of recancel; in the act of cancelling again or revoking a cancellation
examples: The company is recanceling the event due to poor attendance.
Efforts are underway for recanceling the previously canceled workshop.

senses.definition: the act of cancelling again or revoking a cancellation
examples: There was a recancellation of the flight.
The policy allows for recancellation under certain conditions.

senses.definition: a rising or revival after a period of decline or inactivity; a reawakening
examples: The recandescence of interest in vinyl records surprised collectors.
There was a recandescence of hope after the news.

senses.definition: the state of being a candidate again or for a new term or position
examples: Her recandidacy for the senate surprised many voters.
The party approved his recandidacy after renewed support.

senses.definition: to mix or blend with cane sugar or to handle cane in some contexts; this term is rare and may be dialect*
examples: In some old cookbooks, to recane the mixture meant to stir in sugar slowly.
The old farmhand knew how to recane the rafters with cane mats.

senses.definition: past tense of recane; bored connotation or archaic usage
examples: The recaned frame was repaired with new cane.
Old furniture was recaned for revival.

senses.definition: plural of cane; lightweight stems from grasses used in weaving
examples: The matterials included several recanes for weaving.
Cane baskets were made with thin recanes.

senses.definition: the act of replacing the cane in furniture seats or bindings
examples: The chair was undergoing recaning to restore its seat.
Recaning a chair can be time-consuming but rewarding.

senses.definition: to say that a belief or statement is no longer true; to formally withdraw a previous statement
examples: He refused to recant his testimony.
The author later recanted some of the claims in the book.

senses.definition: the act of recanting; a formal withdrawal of a statement or belief
examples: His recantation surprised the courtroom.
The saint's recantation is documented in historical records.

senses.definition: plural of recantation; multiple acts of recanting
examples: There were several recantations during the hearing.
The document lists multiple recantations by witnesses.

senses.definition: past tense of recant; stated that previous beliefs or statements were not true
examples: Witnesses recanted their earlier testimonies.
The scientist recanted the controversial theory after new data.

senses.definition: a person who recants; someone who withdraws a former statement or belief
examples: The recanter admitted changing their stance.
Historically, a recanter faced social penalties.
